title: Export from a dashboard
excerpt: Exporting from a board is more like a report and does not lend itself to analyzing data using Excel as it has header rows, sub totals, etc. While this is good for a PDF style report, it is not so good for an Excel report…

posts.author: srikanthvfm (Srikanth Srinivas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Exporting from a board is more like a report and does not lend itself to analyzing data using Excel as it has header rows, sub totals, etc. While this is good for a PDF style report, it is not so good for an Excel report. It will be good to have an Excel export option that has only 1 header row and has all the detailed rows so that it can be later used to create pivot tables, filters etc. This can be a separate tab - so they can go to the current tab for report format, and a new tab, let us say “Summary Data” Tab if they want to do analysis. Since Excel attachments are not allowed, I have taken screenshots of the current tab and also the recommended summary tab so you can see the difference.

Screenshot of Recommended Summary Tab631×805 227 KB

Screenshot of Current Export478×787 77.7 KB

posts.author: arun.paudyal (Arun Paudyal [Decadis AG])
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Srikanth (@srikanthvfm)

I am Arun, product owner at Decadis AG. Looking at your use case, I believe our app sumUp for monday can provide the solution you are looking for.

(Right click on the gif and select “Open Image in new Tab” for full screen view)

Our app offers two powerful widgets that help you pivot large datasets into one or two dimensions with ease.

You can individually export the data in each widget as a CSV file that is essential for further analysis.

I encourage you to give it a try and see the benefits firsthand. If you have any questions or would like to discuss your specific use case, feel free to reach out to us.

Looking forward to your thoughts!

Best regards,
Arun

posts.author: srikanthvfm (Srikanth Srinivas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for the tip.

I downloaded the App. However, when I added the Widget, I was able to add the Group, but was not able to summarize by Task as rows and weeks as columns with the the sum of the hours of the Effort Spent as the cells.
